description: Setting a function timeout shorter than its slowest downstream call, causing truncated work and retry storms.
body: Failure mode: the function timeout is left at a low default while a downstream API/database call can take longer, so the function is killed mid-call; the event source then retries, repeating partial work. Detection signals: timeouts under load while downstreams are merely slow, not failing; retries amplifying load; partial writes. Do not set timeout below worst-case downstream latency. Safer fallback: measure the slowest downstream call under load, set timeout above it with margin (but bounded), make the handler idempotent so retries are safe, and add a DLQ for genuine failures.
